title: Challenge - Deploying Amazon RDS Multi-AZ and Read Replica, Simulate Failover

tasks.title: Sign in to AWS Management Console

steps.text: Click On The Open Console Button, And You Will Get Redirected To AWS Console In A New Browser Tab.
On The AWS Sign-In Page, Leave The Account ID As Default. Never Edit/Remove The 12 Digit Account ID Present In The AWS Console. Otherwise, You Cannot Proceed With The Challenge.
Now Copy Your User Name and Password In The Lab Console To The IAM Username and Password In AWS Console And Click On The Sign In Button.
Once Signed In To The AWS Management Console, Make The Default AWS Region As US East (N. Virginia) Us-East-1.

In This Challenge, Your AWS Compute Skills Are Put To The Test. You'll Be Given A Requirement And You Have To Reach It Using Your Knowledge Of RDS, EC2 instances and databases. Which Will Be Used In This Challenge. This Challenge Will Help You Understand The Real-Time Scenarios.
Sign in to AWS Management Console

A XYZ company is expanding its infrastructure on the cloud to enhance reliability and scalability. They aim to deploy a robust database solution capable of handling high volumes of user data with minimal downtime. After evaluating various options, they decided to leverage Amazon Aurora with Multi-AZ deployment on AWS for its superior performance, availability, and durability. Your task is to architect and implement an Amazon Aurora with Multi-AZ deployment on AWS to meet these demands efficiently.
Launch an EC2 instance named MyRdsEc2server with Amazon Linux 2023 kernel-6.1 AMI, t2.micro type, and MySSHKey key pair.
Configure the security group for the EC2 instance named MyEC2Server_SG to allow SSH access.
Install MySQL on the EC2 instance using a user data script.
Create a security group for the RDS instance named rds-maz-SG allowing MySQL/Aurora port (3306) from anywhere.
Create an Aurora MySQL database named MyAuroraCluster with Multi-AZ deployment, specifying DB instance size as db.t3.medium, and configuring it to be publicly accessible for connectivity
Connect to the Aurora database and note down the endpoints for Master (Writer) and Reader instances.
Modify the RDS security group to allow access from the EC2 instance.
SSH into the EC2 instance and use MySQL client to interact with the Aurora database.

Perform database operations such as creating a database named labrds, table named lab, with fields mentioned below and insert the data. Fields: subject_id (INT, AUTO_INCREMENT) subject_name (VARCHAR(255), NOT NULL) teacher (VARCHAR(255)) start_date (DATE) lesson (TEXT)
Fields: subject_id (INT, AUTO_INCREMENT) subject_name (VARCHAR(255), NOT NULL) teacher (VARCHAR(255)) start_date (DATE) lesson (TEXT)
subject_id (INT, AUTO_INCREMENT)
subject_name (VARCHAR(255), NOT NULL)
teacher (VARCHAR(255))
start_date (DATE)
lesson (TEXT)
Initiate a failover from the RDS console to test Multi-AZ functionality.
Connect to the new master instance and verify the presence of the database, table, and data.
Click On The Validate To Complete The Challenge.
Sign Out Of The AWS Account.
You Have Successfully Completed The Challenge.
Click On End Challenge Button from the lab console And Wait Till The Process Gets Completed.
